content.problemStatement:
You are given an array of NNN integers.

You can perform the following operation at most KKK times:


Choose 222 indices iii and jjj such that 1‚â§i<j‚â§N1 \le i < j \le N1‚â§i<j‚â§N
Set the value of AjA_jAj‚Äã to AiA_iAi‚Äã.
Find the minimum number of distinct elements that could be left in AAA after performing at most KKK operations.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line contains a single integer NNN.
The second line contains NNN integers - A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äã,A2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äã.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the minimum number of distinct elements after at most KKK operations.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 2‚ãÖ1052 \cdot 10^52‚ãÖ105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 1 : There are no operations to perform since K=2K = 2K=2. The initial array has 444 distinct elements.
Test Case 2 : We can choose (i,j)=(1,3)(i, j) = (1, 3)(i,j)=(1,3) and then (i,j)=(2,4)(i, j) = (2, 4)(i,j)=(2,4) to get the array [1,2,1,2][1, 2, 1, 2][1,2,1,2] which has only 222 distinct elements.
